top_stories.title: Term deposit yield climbs as BSP tightens stance
top_stories.summary: THE CENTRAL BANK’S one-week term deposits fetched a higher average yield on Wednesday, even as demand soared, as the market priced in the latest rate hike and expectations of further policy tightening. The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas’ (BSP) seven-day term deposit facility (TDF) drew P124.436 billion in tenders, more...

top_stories.title: Peso dip offsets losses from tax pause—BOC
top_stories.summary: A weaker peso helped cushion the Bureau of Customs’ (BOC) revenue collections as the local tender’s level jacked up the value of imports and offset the impact of the suspension of the excise tax on kerosene and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).

top_stories.title: Price Tracker: Oil, fuel monitor for Apr. 30
top_stories.summary: Motorists are seeing a third straight week of rollbacks in diesel prices, even as gasoline prices have begun to edge up again.

top_stories.title: PHL’s twin deficits seen worsening on oil shocks
top_stories.summary: A Canadian investment research firm has flagged the country’s fiscal and current account deficits could worsen if oil-supply shocks on the economy continues.

top_stories.title: Central banks ‘scoop up a load’ of gold in bumpy Q1
top_stories.summary: CENTRAL banks added gold holdings at the fastest pace in more than a year in the first quarter, as a slump in prices encouraged a wave of buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions.

top_stories.title: Airfares still high in May despite lower fuel fee
top_stories.summary: Pricey airfares are expected to persist through the first half of May after the Civil Aeronautics Board (CAB) set a Level 18 fuel surcharge for the period, which, while a notch lower than the peak rates imposed in late April, remains more than five times higher than pre-Middle East crisis levels.
